creator: omar.polo@protonmail.com
text:
(In reply to wjenkner from comment #4)

From what I've seen, stack 1.5 has some bugs that were fixed in stack-1.6 (see for example [1]) and if you install stack with their installer[2] you'll get stack 1.6.3.

However, you are completely right about the ghc version, shame on me for not noticing that. But still, I think that it worth to update the makefile so we can build directly from upstream. Correct me if I'm wrong, but we're currently building stack from [3] and that repo has been archived by its owner, so it will never be updated and we'll be unable to build stack version greater than 1.6.1.


[1]: https://github.com/haskell-hvr/cassava/pull/155#issuecomment-333298780
[2]: https://get.haskellstack.org
[3]: https://github.com/applicative-tech/stack-dependencies/
